last_item: pgbouncer — Enable's final IsRunning gate (internal/pgbouncer/enable.go:208-216), the last guard before persisting enabled=true, is the ONLY dead-pooler catch on the idempotent no-config-change re-run (Reload is skipped when nothing changed). The Iter #4 test only drives the first-run/config-CHANGED path, where Reload's own post-apply verify fires first and masks this gate — so deleting the final gate left the whole suite green (proven: if !running -> if false stayed GREEN). Band-1 correctness / Mode-A prove-it: the panel must never record the pooler on over one that is actually down.
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): +2 tests in internal/pgbouncer/enable_test.go, both driving the REAL Enable twice (healthy first run -> FakeRunner.On override of is-active (last responder wins) -> identical no-change re-run so Reload is skipped). (1) _OverDeadPoolerFailsAtFinalGate: is-active 'inactive' -> IsRunning (false,nil) -> the !running arm; asserts CodeInternal + 'did not come up after enable' (uniquely pins enable.go:213 vs Reload's CodeExec/'not running afterward'), Reloaded/ConfigChanged/UserlistChanged==false (Reload skipped), Running==false, setKeys unchanged (no re-persist). (2) _UndeterminableStateFailsAtFinalGate: is-active empty-stdout+runner-err -> IsRunning (false,err) -> the err!=nil arm; asserts CodeExec + 'could not determine service state' and NOT 'did not come up'. Both arms of the gate are correct -> no bug; tests lock the contract. Mutation-proven (perl apply + git checkout, tree clean each): if !running->if false (first test RED, pre-existing suite GREEN = proves the hole was real), ->if running, InternalError->ExecError, gate-message change, if res.Reloaded && !running -> all RED. test-skeptic found a REAL escape: the first test only ever drives IsRunning's (false,nil) branch, so running, err := -> running, _ := (swallow the transport error; it COMPILES because a stale err from an earlier := stays in scope as dead code) survived. CLOSED before commit by adding the error-arm companion test; re-mutated -> RED at the CodeExec assert. code-reviewer clean (FakeRunner override verified to shadow correctly; no other command contains 'is-active'; CodeInternal vs CodeExec distinct; setKeys count non-tautological). Backend gate green: gofmt clean, go vet OK, go test ./... exit 0 (0 FAIL), static CGO_ENABLED=0 build (cmd/indiepg) OK. Web untouched. e2e N/A (pure unit + Docker unavailable).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
